"Ephedra" is a genus of plants that are also known as jointfir or joint pine.

Ephedra is a plant that is native to Asia and North America, and it is known for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Ephedra include its ability to aid in weight loss, improve respiratory function, and boost energy levels due to its stimulant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ephedra include ephedrine, pseudoephedrine, and other alkaloids that contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Ephedra include tea, capsules, and tinctures, which are often used to aid in weight loss and improve respiratory function.
